sections.context:
Sapling currently reaches *into* overstory: src/loop.ts:144-164 spawns `ov mail check` after every turn, and src/loop.ts:204-267 spawns `ov mail send` on exit. src/cli.ts:139-146 also reads OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME / OVERSTORY_TASK_ID / OVERSTORY_METRICS_PATH env vars as fallbacks. Comments throughout reference `ov inspect`, `ov stop`, and 'overstory lastActivity heartbeat'. A test file is named src/overstory-e2e.test.ts.

This matters because (a) overstory is becoming antiquated and we don't want sapling pinned to its lifecycle, and (b) the active subprocess calls invert the dependency direction — the orchestrator should drive sapling, not the other way around. Sapling already has a clean general-purpose orchestration surface (NDJSON events on stdout via --json, JSON-RPC stdin steering via --mode rpc, Unix socket state queries via --rpc-socket, generic metrics-path output, generic guards/hooks). Removing the active overstory calls leaves that surface intact and orchestrator-agnostic. The audit (2026-05-07) confirmed there is no npm dep, no overstory imports, no layout assumptions in config.ts, and no overstory mention in README — coupling is localized to ~5 files.
sections.behavior_invariant:
Sapling's externally observable behavior must remain identical for every consumer that uses the documented general-purpose surface:

1. Standalone `sp run <prompt>` produces the same output, exit codes, and side effects as before.
2. `--json` emits the same NDJSON event shape (turn_start, turn_complete, tool_call, tool_result, status, etc.) on the same channel.
3. `--mode rpc` accepts the same JSON-RPC methods (`steer`, `followUp`, `abort`) with the same semantics.
4. `--rpc-socket <path>` exposes the same `getState` query API.
5. `--guards-file <path>` enforces the same guard types (blockedTools, readOnly, pathBoundary, fileScope, blockedBashPatterns) with identical semantics.
6. `--metrics-path <path>` writes the same metrics JSON shape on exit.
7. `--agent-name` and `--task-id` flags continue to label events/metrics identically; only the OVERSTORY_*-named env fallbacks are removed (SAPLING_AGENT_NAME / SAPLING_TASK_ID / SAPLING_METRICS_PATH remain).
8. Existing `src/overstory-e2e.test.ts` scenarios (guard enforcement, NDJSON event shape, RPC socket state) continue to pass against the renamed test file with identical assertions.
9. An orchestrator that wires up the surface above (consume NDJSON, push RPC steers at turn boundaries, watch the metrics file) can reproduce the prior overstory→sapling end-to-end behavior with no sapling-side code.

What MAY change: sapling stops invoking `ov mail` subprocesses, stops reading OVERSTORY_* env vars, and stops naming overstory in source/tests/comments. Any orchestrator (including overstory) that depended on sapling actively shelling out must migrate to the push model.
sections.approach:
Lean deletion, not abstraction. The audit shows sapling already has the general-purpose orchestration surface needed (NDJSON + RPC + metrics-path + guards). The right move is to *remove* the overstory-reaching code, not introduce a new lifecycle-hook abstraction layer.

Strategy: (1) verify the existing surface covers everything `ov mail` was doing, (2) delete the `ov mail check` turn-end poll and the `ov mail send` exit call, (3) delete the OVERSTORY_* env fallbacks, (4) rename the overstory-named test file and rewrite assertions in orchestrator-agnostic terms, (5) scrub overstory-named comments and update CLAUDE.md to describe the orchestrator surface generically, (6) ship a short migration note documenting what overstory (or any orchestrator) must wire up post-decoupling.

Why not a `--lifecycle-hook` flag: it would re-couple sapling to the *idea* of an orchestrator, just generically. The existing RPC + NDJSON surface is already the right interface — we just need to commit to it as the only one. If a future use case emerges that genuinely needs subprocess hooks (non-RPC consumers), revisit then.

Sequencing risk: step 2 (deleting `ov mail check`) breaks any live overstory→sapling flow until overstory migrates to RPC push. We accept that — the user has signaled overstory is on its way out. No deprecation period.

steps.title: Audit the ov-mail surface against existing RPC + NDJSON
steps.description: Read src/loop.ts:144-164 (checkEcosystemMail) and src/loop.ts:204-267 (handleEcosystemExit) carefully. Document exactly what data flows in/out of `ov mail check` and `ov mail send`. For each piece of data, identify the equivalent on the existing general-purpose surface (NDJSON event field, RPC method, metrics file field). Output: a short table in the seed body mapping ov-mail behavior → general surface equivalent. If any gap is found (something ov-mail does that the general surface cannot), surface it before proceeding to deletion — that gap would change the plan.

steps.title: Delete checkEcosystemMail() turn-end poll
steps.description: Remove the `ov mail check` subprocess invocation from src/loop.ts:144-164. Remove the function definition and its call site at the turn boundary. Remove any imports/types that become unused. The orchestrator now must push steers via RPC instead of having sapling pull. Run `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` — expect src/overstory-e2e.test.ts to still pass since it doesn't depend on the outbound poll.

steps.title: Delete handleEcosystemExit() ov-mail-send call
steps.description: Remove the `ov mail send task_done` subprocess invocation from src/loop.ts:204-267. Keep the metrics file write — that's a generic feature behind --metrics-path and is part of the preserved surface. Remove any types/state that exist only to support the mail-send call. Run quality gate.

steps.title: Drop OVERSTORY_* env var fallbacks
steps.description: In src/cli.ts:139-146 (and anywhere else the audit surfaces), remove fallback reads of OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME, OVERSTORY_TASK_ID, OVERSTORY_METRICS_PATH. Keep only SAPLING_AGENT_NAME, SAPLING_TASK_ID, SAPLING_METRICS_PATH. Update src/types.ts EcosystemConfig if any field exists solely to mirror an OVERSTORY_* var. Run quality gate.

steps.title: Rename overstory-e2e test file and rewrite assertions in orchestrator-agnostic terms
steps.description: Rename src/overstory-e2e.test.ts → src/orchestrator-surface.test.ts (or similar — confirm name during implementation). Rewrite assertions to test 'a guards.json with the documented schema enforces these rules', 'the NDJSON stream emits these fields', 'the RPC socket answers getState with this shape', 'the metrics-path file contains these fields' — all without naming overstory. Make sure coverage is preserved 1:1: the tests must catch the same regressions they caught before. Run quality gate.

steps.title: Scrub overstory-named comments and rename internal symbols if misleading
steps.description: Grep `src/`, `agents/`, and any TS source for 'overstory' / 'ov inspect' / 'ov stop' / 'ov mail' / 'ovrstory'. Replace each with orchestrator-agnostic phrasing or delete the comment if it no longer applies after the deletions. Decide whether `EcosystemConfig` / `EventConfig` type names still read well; if `EcosystemConfig` was 'overstory config in disguise', consider `OrchestratorConfig` — but only rename if the existing name is genuinely misleading after the deletions (avoid churn). Acceptance: `grep -rin overstory src/ agents/` returns zero hits. Run quality gate.

steps.title: Update CLAUDE.md and README to describe the orchestrator surface generically
steps.description: In sapling/CLAUDE.md, remove or rephrase any framing that positions sapling as 'overstory's worker'. Add (or expand) a section titled 'Orchestrator integration surface' that documents: NDJSON event stream (--json), JSON-RPC stdin (--mode rpc), Unix socket state queries (--rpc-socket), guards/hooks (--guards-file), metrics output (--metrics-path), agent/task labeling (--agent-name, --task-id, env equivalents). Keep README.md's existing standalone framing. The single line in CLAUDE.md noting sapling is part of the os-eco ecosystem may stay (it's accurate, not coupling).

steps.title: Write migration note for orchestrator consumers
steps.description: Add a short doc (e.g., docs/orchestrator-migration.md or a section in CLAUDE.md) describing what an orchestrator that previously relied on sapling's `ov mail` calls must now do: subscribe to NDJSON events to detect turn boundaries, push steers via RPC instead of waiting for sapling to poll, read the metrics file on sapling exit instead of receiving a task_done message. One worked example showing a minimal orchestrator stub (~30 lines of pseudocode) is enough. File a follow-up seed in the overstory repo (or root .seeds/ for cross-repo) tracking the overstory-side migration; do not modify overstory in this plan.

steps.title: Final verification: standalone smoke test + full quality gate
steps.description: Run `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` — must be green. Run a standalone `sp run 'say hello'` smoke test and confirm output, exit code, and any --json event stream match a pre-decoupling baseline (capture before starting step 2). Run `grep -rin overstory src/ agents/` and confirm zero hits. Run `grep -rin OVERSTORY_ src/` and confirm zero hits. If any check fails, fix before closing the parent seed.

acceptance: `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` is green at HEAD after all steps land.
`grep -rin overstory src/ agents/` returns zero hits (the single CLAUDE.md ecosystem-membership line is allowed and is documentation, not coupling).
Standalone `sp run <prompt>` produces output and exit code identical to a pre-decoupling baseline captured in step 1.
The renamed orchestrator-surface test file asserts the same behaviors the prior overstory-e2e test asserted, with no loss of coverage.

An orchestrator wiring up only the documented surface (NDJSON events + RPC steers + metrics-path file + guards-file) can reproduce the prior overstory→sapling end-to-end flow without any sapling-side code change. Verified by writing the ~30-line pseudocode example in the migration doc and walking through it against the surface.
A migration note exists explaining what overstory (or any orchestrator) must change post-decoupling, and a follow-up seed tracks the overstory-side work.

sections.context: Warren V2 (warren ROADMAP item R-07) wants to surface sapling's differentiating runtime signals — context health, compaction events, per-turn relevance scores, pending commitments — as the visible reason to use sapling over claude-code. Today the pipeline emits zero internal events. Compaction is invisible (consumers must infer it from `archiveEntryCount` deltas in turn_end). Operation scores never leave the pipeline (evaluate.ts:233 sets op.score in-place; nothing reads it externally). Commitments are extracted (ingest.ts:141 extractCommitments) but stored only as strings on TurnMetadata.commitments and rendered into the system-prompt archive — they are not addressable, queryable, or observable. Verbose-mode pipeline reasoning goes to stderr only (see registry.ts:117/132/147/160/190 console.error blocks); consumers running `--mode rpc + --json` cannot see it. The fix is to extend the existing NDJSON event stream and getState RPC with structured signals from the pipeline's compact, evaluate, and ingest stages, plus a verbose-gated structured replacement for the stderr stage logs. All additions are additive on the existing event schema (consumers ignoring unknown event types or fields keep working).
sections.approach:
Lean additive extension of three existing surfaces — EventEmitter (src/hooks/events.ts), StageContext (src/context/v1/types.ts:360), and PipelineRpcState (src/rpc/types.ts:34) — plus one schema change inside ingest.ts to give commitments stable IDs. No new abstractions, no new transports. Each pipeline stage that has interesting decisions to surface (compact, budget, ingest, plus all five for verbose mode) emits a structured event through an EventEmitter threaded into StageContext. The loop already owns an EventEmitter (src/loop.ts:279, 318, 582); we extend PipelineOptions with an optional eventEmitter + a per-turn currentTurn updater, and `SaplingPipelineV1.process()` writes both into ctx before calling `registry.run(ctx)`. Stages remain pure — they read ctx.eventEmitter just like they read ctx.verbose today.

Key design call: the seed proposes `turn_end.score` but evaluate.ts scores operations, not turns. We surface `activeOperationId` and `activeOperationScore` on `turn_end` so the semantics are unambiguous; warren can render 'turn 7 was inside operation #3 (score 0.78)'. Keep the `score` alias for the seed's wording but document it as the active-operation score.

Key design call: commitments need stable IDs to make `commitment_added` / `commitment_resolved` events correlatable. We change `TurnMetadata.commitments` from `string[]` to `{ id: string, text: string }[]` (id = `c-<turn>-<n>` deterministic), thread the new shape through ingest's extractCommitments + computePendingCommitments + templates.ts renderCompactSummary, and add a per-pipeline commitments registry that tracks pending vs resolved status so the resolved heuristic (commitment's mentioned files become artifacts of a later operation) can fire against a known set. This also unlocks the RPC `getState` extension: `pipeline.commitments: [{ id, turn, text, status }]`.

Key design call: `pipeline_stage` events fire only under --verbose (matches the existing stderr behavior; production warren consumers won't be flooded). We replace each stage's stderr log with a structured emit and keep the stderr line in addition for human debugging. Reason taxonomy for `compact` events is grounded in the actual code paths: `score_below_threshold` (compact.ts:230 `op.score < threshold` → compactOperation), `budget_pressure` (budget.ts:270 ops moved to status=archived because they don't fit), and `tool_output_truncated` (compact.ts truncateOperationOutputs on a kept op). The seed's `fifo_overflow` is dropped — there is no FIFO eviction in the current budget code; archival is score-based, not order-based.

alternatives.name: Single discriminated `pipeline_event` type with a `subtype` field
alternatives.rejected_because: Less ergonomic for consumers — they'd have to switch on `event.type === 'pipeline_event' && event.subtype === 'compact'` instead of the existing `event.type === 'compact'` pattern matched against `tool_start` / `tool_end`. Deviates from the established event vocabulary in events.ts.

alternatives.name: Push pipeline signals through the existing eventConfig argv-hooks system (onToolStart/onToolEnd/onSessionEnd in guards.json)
alternatives.rejected_because: Hooks are subprocess fire-and-forget designed for guard enforcement, not live UI. Warren needs structured stdout it can stream into a UI panel; spawning a subprocess per pipeline event would be expensive and lose ordering with other NDJSON events.

alternatives.name: Emit signals as deltas on `turn_end` (e.g., `turn_end.compactedOperations: [{id, score}], turn_end.newCommitments: [...]`)
alternatives.rejected_because: Loses per-event ordering and timing. Warren wants to render 'compacted op #3 mid-turn because score dropped to 0.18' as a discrete entry in a feed; collapsing into turn_end forces consumers to diff against the previous turn_end and reconstruct what happened. Also conflates pipeline decisions with turn-level token accounting.

alternatives.name: Have SaplingPipelineV1.process() return a list of events for the loop to emit
alternatives.rejected_because: Forces the loop to know the shape of every pipeline event and proxy them; couples loop.ts to pipeline-internal types. Threading the EventEmitter through StageContext keeps stages self-contained — they emit what they know. Mirrors the existing `ctx.verbose` pattern.

steps.title: Plumbing: thread EventEmitter + currentTurn through StageContext and PipelineOptions
steps.description: Foundation step that every event-emitting stage depends on. (1) Add `eventEmitter?: EventEmitter` and `currentTurn?: number` to `StageContext` in src/context/v1/types.ts:360. Use a structural type (not a hard import of src/hooks/events.ts) to keep the v1 module orchestrator-agnostic — define `PipelineEventSink` interface in types.ts with a single `emit(event: Record<string, unknown>): void` method; EventEmitter satisfies it structurally. (2) Add `eventSink?: PipelineEventSink` to `PipelineOptions` in src/context/v1/pipeline.ts:31. Store as private field. (3) In `SaplingPipelineV1.process()` (src/context/v1/pipeline.ts:77), populate `ctx.eventEmitter = this.eventSink` and `ctx.currentTurn = input.turnHint.turn`. (4) In src/loop.ts:265, pass `eventSink: options.eventEmitter` when constructing the pipeline. (5) Add a unit test in src/context/v1/pipeline.test.ts asserting that a stub event sink receives no events when no stage emits (baseline) and that ctx.eventEmitter is populated on the StageContext passed to stages. No behavior change for existing consumers.

steps.title: Compact events: emit `compact` with reason taxonomy from compact + budget stages
steps.description: Wire the `compact` event into the two code paths that change op.status away from active/completed: (1) In src/context/v1/compact.ts compact() at line 230, when `op.score < threshold` and we call compactOperation(op), emit `{ type: 'compact', turn: ctx.currentTurn, operationId: op.id, reason: 'score_below_threshold', score: op.score, archivedAs: 'summary', summaryLength: op.summary?.length ?? 0 }`. The compact() function will need ctx (or eventSink + currentTurn) passed in — extend its signature, update registry.ts compactStage to pass ctx through. (2) In src/context/v1/budget.ts at line 270 (where `op.status = 'archived'` is set in the loop over result.archived), emit `{ type: 'compact', turn, operationId: op.id, reason: 'budget_pressure', score: op.score, budgetUtilization: ctx.budgetUtil.utilization, archivedAs: 'archived' }`. Same ctx-passing pattern. (3) Optionally add `tool_output_truncated` events from truncateOperationOutputs() — defer to follow-up unless the implementer can do it cleanly without bloating the loop; the seed flagged this as 'maybe also'. (4) Drop the seed's `fifo_overflow` value from the taxonomy — no FIFO eviction exists in budget.ts. (5) Add unit tests covering both reasons and verifying no event fires when no compaction happens. Document the reason enum in src/hooks/events.ts JSDoc.

steps.title: Extend `turn_end` with `activeOperationId` and `activeOperationScore` (alias `score`)
steps.description: The seed asks for `turn_end.score` but evaluate.ts scores operations, not turns. Surface unambiguous fields and keep the alias. (1) In src/loop.ts:582 where turn_end is emitted, read the active op from `pipeline.getState()` (already called at line 393 elsewhere) and add `activeOperationId: state?.activeOperationId ?? null` and `activeOperationScore: activeOp?.score ?? null` plus alias `score: activeOp?.score ?? null`. (2) Add the new fields to `EventEmitter.turnEnd()` in src/hooks/events.ts:88 (extend signature with optional params; keep backward-compatible call from loop.ts). (3) Update JSDoc comment to explain `score` semantics: 'score of the operation containing this turn at the moment turn_end fires; null when no active operation; recomputed every turn by the evaluate stage'. (4) Add e2e assertion in src/orchestrator-surface.test.ts that turn_end events include numeric `score` and `activeOperationId` after at least one turn has been ingested. No new event type; this is purely a payload extension.

steps.title: `pipeline_stage` events under --verbose (structured replacement for stderr logs)
steps.description: Replace the five `if (ctx.verbose) console.error(...)` blocks in registry.ts (lines 117, 132, 147, 160, 190) with both the existing stderr line AND a structured emit gated on `ctx.verbose && ctx.eventEmitter`. Event shape: `{ type: 'pipeline_stage', turn: ctx.currentTurn, stage: '<name>', metadata: { ... } }` where metadata mirrors the data each stage already logs (ingest: opCount, activeOpId, turns; evaluate: per-op scores; compact: compactedCount; budget: utilization, archived; render: msgCount, archiveCount). Drop the seed's `decision: 'kept'|'compacted'|'truncated'` field — it doesn't map cleanly to all five stages and the per-op compaction decision is already covered by the `compact` event from step 2. Keep stderr lines unchanged so human debugging via `--verbose` without `--json` is unaffected. Add a unit test in src/context/v1/pipeline.test.ts asserting five pipeline_stage events fire per process() call when verbose=true + eventSink is wired, and zero events when verbose=false. Note in CLAUDE.md / orchestrator-migration.md that pipeline_stage events are verbose-gated (production warren should not enable --verbose).

steps.title: Commitment IDs: change schema, add registry, emit `commitment_added` / `commitment_resolved`
steps.description: Largest step. Commitments today are bare strings (TurnMetadata.commitments: string[], Operation.pendingCommitments: string[]). To emit add/resolve events we need stable IDs and a tracking registry. (1) Change shape in src/context/v1/types.ts: `TurnMetadata.commitments?: { id: string, text: string }[]` and `Operation.pendingCommitments?: { id: string, text: string }[]`. ID format: `c-<turnIndex>-<i>` deterministic per turn (e.g., `c-7-0`, `c-7-1`). (2) Update extractCommitments() in src/context/v1/ingest.ts:141 to take turnIndex and emit objects with IDs. Update extractTurnMetadata() (line 236) to call it with turn.index. (3) Update computePendingCommitments() (line 189) — extractFilesFromCommitment now reads commitment.text. Update renderCompactSummary in src/context/v1/templates.ts (the 'Pending: item1; item2' line) to read .text. (4) Add a `commitmentRegistry: Map<string, { turn: number, text: string, status: 'pending'|'resolved', resolvedBy?: string }>` field to SaplingPipelineV1. Track adds (new IDs not in registry → emit `{ type: 'commitment_added', turn, commitmentId, text }`) and resolves (a later operation's artifacts include all files mentioned in a pending commitment → emit `{ type: 'commitment_resolved', turn, commitmentId, resolvedBy: '<tool>:<file>' }`). Resolution check runs at end of ingest stage. (5) Migrate the persisted pipeline-state file format (per archive-persistence-design.md) — bump a schema version or coerce old string[] entries on read by synthesizing IDs. (6) Update existing tests in src/context/v1/ingest.test.ts and templates.test.ts for the new shape. Add new tests for the registry, add events, resolve heuristic. This step is large enough that a follow-up sub-plan via `sd plan submit` on the spawned child seed may be warranted.

steps.title: RPC `getState` extension: include pending commitments
steps.description: Depends on step 5's commitment registry. (1) Extend `PipelineRpcState` in src/rpc/types.ts:34 with `commitments?: { id: string, turn: number, text: string, status: 'pending' | 'resolved' }[]`. (2) Extend `SaplingPipelineV1.getRpcState()` (src/context/v1/pipeline.ts:126) to read from the commitment registry and include up to N most recent pending commitments (cap at 50 to keep RPC responses small). Resolved commitments not included in default response per the seed's 'pending commitments panel' framing — add as future extension if needed. (3) Update src/rpc/socket.test.ts and src/rpc/server.test.ts assertions to cover the new field. (4) Document in docs/orchestrator-migration.md that `getState.pipeline.commitments` is now part of the response shape. No new RPC method (the seed considered `getCommitments` and chose to ride on getState — followed here).

steps.title: Documentation, schema reference, and orchestrator-surface e2e tests
steps.description: Final step that ties everything together. (1) Create `docs/event-schema.md` (or extend the existing JSDoc in src/hooks/events.ts and link to it from docs/orchestrator-migration.md) documenting every event type — existing (ready, turn_start, tool_start, tool_end, turn_end, progress, result, error) plus new (compact, pipeline_stage, commitment_added, commitment_resolved) — with full payload shapes, when each fires, and which CLI flags gate it. Highlight that `pipeline_stage` is `--verbose`-gated. (2) Update CLAUDE.md 'Orchestrator integration surface' section to mention the new events and the extended getState response. (3) Add e2e assertions to src/orchestrator-surface.test.ts: run a synthetic agent loop long enough to trigger compaction (long bench scenario or seeded message array), assert the NDJSON stream contains at least one `compact` event with a valid `reason`, at least one `commitment_added` followed by a matching `commitment_resolved`, `turn_end` events with numeric `score` and `activeOperationId`, and (when --verbose set) `pipeline_stage` events for all five stages per turn. (4) Run `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`. (5) File a follow-up seed in the warren repo (or root .seeds/ for cross-repo) tracking warren-side consumption of the new events; do not modify warren in this plan.

risks: Commitment schema migration breaks the persisted `.sapling/pipeline-state.<agent>.json` format (archive-persistence-design.md) for any in-flight session — must coerce old string[] entries on read by synthesizing IDs, or bump a schema version field. Failing to handle this loses pending-commitment state across restarts.

Active-operation score on turn_end is recomputed every turn by evaluate.ts; a turn that ends inside a compacted operation will report the active op's score (which may be a fresh op with score=1.0), not a 'score for this turn'. Could mislead consumers who treat it as historical. Mitigate by clear JSDoc and naming the unambiguous field `activeOperationScore`.

Commitment resolution heuristic ('commitment's mentioned files become artifacts of a later operation') may fire false positives — a file getting touched for an unrelated reason will mark a commitment resolved. Conservative fix: require the resolution to happen in a different operation than the one that introduced the commitment, and log resolvedBy with the specific tool+file so consumers can spot-check.

Pipeline_stage events under verbose can be very chatty: 5 stages × N turns × multi-op evaluate logs = high event volume. If a consumer accidentally enables --verbose with --json and pipes to a slow NDJSON parser, this could backpressure stdout. Mitigate by capping evaluate's per-op metadata to the top-K scored ops (e.g., first 10), not all of them.

Backwards compat: any consumer with strict event-type validation (rejects unknown types) breaks. Pre-1.0 OK per the seed, but warren V1 (if any) and any external NDJSON parser must be checked. Confirm by grep'ing warren and any docs that promise event-type whitelists.

Threading EventEmitter through StageContext via a structural PipelineEventSink interface (rather than importing EventEmitter directly) is necessary to keep src/context/v1/ orchestrator-agnostic per the existing module boundaries — forgetting this would create an undesired src/context → src/hooks dep cycle.

Tool output truncation events (the seed's optional `tool_output_truncated` reason) fire per turn × per tool result, much higher cardinality than per-op compact events. Defer to follow-up unless cleanly gated, otherwise risks event-stream bloat similar to pipeline_stage.
acceptance: `compact` events fire whenever an op's status moves to `compacted` (compact stage) or `archived` (budget stage), with `reason` ∈ {`score_below_threshold`, `budget_pressure`} and the documented payload (turn, operationId, score, archivedAs, plus stage-specific fields). Verified by unit tests on compact.ts and budget.ts plus an e2e assertion in orchestrator-surface.test.ts.

`turn_end` events include `activeOperationId` (number | null), `activeOperationScore` (number | null), and `score` alias. Verified by an e2e assertion that runs the loop for ≥3 turns and checks every turn_end payload.

`pipeline_stage` events fire under `--verbose` for all five stages (ingest, evaluate, compact, budget, render) per process() call, with stage-specific metadata. Zero pipeline_stage events fire when --verbose is off. Stderr verbose log lines remain unchanged for human debugging. Verified by pipeline.test.ts.

`commitment_added` fires once per new commitment with stable `commitmentId` (deterministic format `c-<turn>-<n>`), and `commitment_resolved` fires when a later operation's artifacts cover all files mentioned in the commitment, with `resolvedBy` populated. Verified by ingest.test.ts and an e2e scenario.
`getState` RPC response (both stdin RPC and Unix socket) includes `pipeline.commitments: [{ id, turn, text, status }]` capped at 50 entries. Verified by server.test.ts and socket.test.ts.

Persisted pipeline-state file (per archive-persistence-design.md) migrates cleanly from the old `commitments: string[]` shape to the new `{ id, text }[]` shape on read — old sessions resume without crashing.

`docs/event-schema.md` (or equivalent JSDoc + cross-link in orchestrator-migration.md) documents every NDJSON event type with payload shape, fire conditions, and CLI gating. CLAUDE.md 'Orchestrator integration surface' section references it.
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` is green at HEAD after all steps land, with no skipped tests.
A follow-up seed exists tracking warren-side consumption of the new events; sapling-side work in this plan is independent of warren landing its consumer.

Existing NDJSON consumers that ignore unknown event types or fields are unaffected — verified by checking that no existing test in orchestrator-surface.test.ts breaks without modification beyond extending assertions.
